memes.label: Arrow
memes.description: The Navajo arrow, companion to the bow, used for hunting and warfare, and carrying symbolic weight in Navajo cosmology and the metaphors of Laughing Boy.

memes.label: Concho Belt
memes.description: Navajo silver concho belt — ornamental leather belt with silver concho disks, a premier form of Navajo silversmithing and personal adornment.

memes.label: Bow
memes.description: The Navajo bow, a primary hunting and warfare weapon crafted from juniper or oak, central to Laughing Boy's identity as a warrior and provider.

memes.description: A Navajo silver and turquoise wrist guard worn during archery — both

memes.label: Silver Bracelet
memes.description: Navajo silver bracelet — a hand-forged silver wrist ornament, often set with turquoise, representing the most personal form of Navajo jewelry.

memes.label: Corn
memes.description: Corn (maize), the staple food of the Navajo, central to daily meals, ceremonial offerings, and the agricultural rhythm of Navajo life in Laughing Boy.

memes.label: "Going Back to the Blanket"
memes.description: "The phrase describing a Navajo person's return to traditional ways after exposure to American culture — Slim Girl's declared project in Laughing Boy, signifying cultural reclamation and the rejection of assimilation"

memes.label: "The Hidden Hogan"
memes.description: "The secret hogan where Laughing Boy and Slim Girl establish their married life — a secluded dwelling symbolizing Slim Girl's attempt to build a protected Navajo life away from American influence"

memes.label: "Horse Race at Tsé Lani"
memes.description: "The competitive horse races at the Tsé Lani gathering — high-stakes events of gambling, honor, and masculine display that frame the novel's opening"

memes.label: "Hozoji (Walking in Beauty)"
memes.description: "The central Navajo philosophical and spiritual concept of 'walking in beauty' — living in harmony, balance, and aesthetic wholeness with the world, woven throughout Oliver La Farge's Laughing Boy"

memes.label: "Jesting Squaw's Son"
memes.description: "Laughing Boy's closest friend and traveling companion — a young Navajo man whose joking manner, gambling spirit, and loyalty embody the codes of Navajo male friendship in the novel"

memes.label: Jewelry
memes.description: Navajo silver and turquoise jewelry — bracelets, belts, necklaces, and ornaments — a primary art form, store of wealth, and marker of status in Laughing Boy.

memes.label: "Laughing Boy"
memes.description: "The protagonist of Oliver La Farge's 1929 novel — a young Navajo silversmith and singer-in-training whose journey from traditional life through love and tragedy embodies the collision of Navajo and American worlds"

memes.label: "Navajo Courting Customs"
memes.description: "The traditional Navajo courtship practice of blanket-courting — the ritualized exchange between young men and women at dances, where a girl pulls a man's blanket to signal romantic interest in Laughing Boy"

memes.label: "Navajo Gambling Culture"
memes.description: "The traditional Navajo practice of betting on horse races, wrestling matches, and moccasin games — a social institution combining sport, risk, honor, and economic exchange in Laughing Boy"

memes.label: "Navajo Horse Culture"
memes.description: "The central role of horses in Navajo life — as transportation, wealth, racing sport, and markers of identity — depicted throughout Oliver La Farge's Laughing Boy"

memes.label: "Navajo Hospitality Customs"
memes.description: "The Navajo customs of food-sharing, guest-welcoming, and gift-giving that structure social relations — the ethic of generosity and reciprocity depicted throughout the gatherings in Laughing Boy"

memes.label: "Slim Girl"

memes.label: "Tsé Lani"
memes.description: "The location of the great dance and horse-racing gathering in the opening chapters of Laughing Boy — a ceremonial and social center of Navajo life"

memes.label: "Tsé Lani Dance"
memes.description: "The multi-night ceremonial dance and gathering at Tsé Lani — the novel's opening event, combining religious ceremony, courtship, horse racing, and community celebration"
